calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-19-2010 Assigned To Trial Court

SHORT CAUSE TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-19-2010 Dismissal - No Appearance
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned from the Presiding Judge Dept. 206 for Court Trial/Settlement Conference. Commissioner: William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Lois Jones, CSR #3689

Plaintiff Clifford S. Daniels was present in pro per, placed under oath and examined.
Robert D. Sanford, Esq. appeared for defendant San Francisco Autoreturn, sued as Auto Return.
Dan Scanlan, Vice President of Towing & Quality Assurance for plaintiff, was placed under oath and examined.
Notice of Time and Place of Trial was filed on September 25, 2009.

Settlement agreement reached by the parties was placed on the record by Mr. Sanford. In accordance with the terms of the settlement, plaintiff Clifford S. Daniels shall dismiss his complaint, with prejudice, within ten of the date of this settlement agreement. In exchange, defendant San Francisco Autoreturn, sued as Auto Return, shall waive all costs, including the sanctions in the amount of $475.00, issued November 23, 2009. The parties hereby waive their rights under Civil Code Section 1542.

Plaintiff Clifford S. Daniels was voir dired by the Court as to the terms and his understanding of this agreement.

Agreement of the parties was accepted by the Court. Dismissal with prejudice was filed today, January 19, 2010.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Sanctions;
Off Calendar Nov-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Robert D. Sanford, Atty. for defendant and moving party: Auto Return
No appearances made by Plantiff

RULING: Defendant San Francisco Auto Return's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses is withdrawn. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Otion For Deemed Admissions And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Robert D. Sanford, Atty. for defendant and moving party: Auto Return
No appearances made by Plantiff

RULING: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendant San Francisco Auto Return's Motion For Deemed Admissions is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant's Request For Admissions are deemed admitted. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $475, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order was signed in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.
